name: Coma-Ruga and GR 92 Loop
name_original: Passeig Marítim de Coma-Ruga a Carrer Lleure a Camí de la Costa Roja — circular desde Coma-Ruga
description: Hike a moderate 5.5-mile (8.9 km) circular route in Coma-Ruga, featuring the coastal promenade and inland paths with 291 feet (89 metres) of
region_intro: Hiking around El Vendrell offers diverse landscapes, from a 7-kilometer coastline with sandy beaches and natural reserves to the rugged terrain of the Serra del Montmell mountain range. The region features protected areas like Les Madrigueres Nature Reserve, known for sand dunes and lagoons, and inland areas with vineyards and historical sites. Hikers can explore coastal paths, Mediterranean woodlands, and higher elevations providing panoramic views. The area also includes the scenic Foix Reservoir, adding variety to the natural environment. Best hiking trails around El Vendrell The most popular hiking route is La Muga Castle Loop, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete, exploring the Bosc de Les Comes and the ruins of Castillo de la Muga. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Vendrell to Puig del Lleo — circular via Sender Pedragosa, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.5 km) path winding through varied inland landscapes and offering elevated views. Local hikers also love the Pavelló Camp d'Esports El Vendrell to Turó del Telègraf — circular, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail leading through inland hills and vineyards, often completed in about 3 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around El Vendrell is defined by coastal paths, the Serra del Montmell mountain range, and natural reserves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal strolls to more demanding inland ascents. The routes in El Vendrell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore El Vendrell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in El Vendrell?
A: El Vendrell offers a diverse range of landscapes. You can explore a 7-kilometer coastline with fine sandy beaches like Sant Salvador and Coma-ruga, along with unique natural reserves such as Les Madrigueres, featuring sand dunes and lagoons. Inland, the rugged terrain of the Serra del Montmell mountain range provides panoramic views, while areas around the Foix Reservoir offer scenic vistas and opportunities to observe waterfowl.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families in El Vendrell?
A: Yes, El Vendrell has numerous easy routes. Approximately 36 of the available hiking tours are rated as easy. A great option for a gentle walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1344333627/la-muga-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Muga Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.8 miles (6.1 km) long and explores the Bosc de Les Comes and the ruins of Castillo de la Muga.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I discover along the trails?
A: Hiking trails in El Vendrell lead to various natural and historical attractions. Along the coast, you can visit the protected Les Madrigueres Nature Reserve, known for its birdlife, or the unique Estany i Riuet de Coma-ruga natural spring. Inland, the Serra del Montmell features the ruins of the medieval Castellot, the Romanesque Sant Miquel Hermitage, and the prominent Montmell Cross. The scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4019675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable attraction, often explored alongside &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in El Vendrell?
A: Yes, many of the trails in El Vendrell are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343813408/el-vendrell-to-puig-del-lleo-circular-via-sender-pedragosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Vendrell to Puig del Lleo — circular via Sender Pedragosa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.4-mile (13.5 km) path winding through varied inland landscapes. Another popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343802379/pavello-camp-d-esports-el-vendrell-to-turo-del-telegraf-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pavelló Camp d'Esports El Vendrell to Turó del Telègraf — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through inland hills and vineyards.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in El Vendrell?
A: The hiking routes in El Vendrell are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse scenery, from coastal paths to rugged mountain terrain, and the well-maintained network of trails that cater to various fitness levels.

Q: Can I find trails that combine coastal views with inland exploration?
A: Absolutely. El Vendrell's unique geography allows for routes that transition between its 7-kilometer coastline and the inland areas. You can explore coastal paths that pass through natural reserves and beaches, then venture slightly inland to discover Mediterranean woodlands, olive groves, and historical sites. The GR 92 long-distance path also passes through the area, offering sections that connect coastal and inland landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific trails that are particularly scenic or offer great views?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider hiking in the Serra del Montmell. The Puig de la Talaia, the highest peak in the Baix Penedès region, offers 360-degree vistas of vineyards, villages, and the sea on clear days. Coastal routes also provide stunning views of the Mediterranean, especially around areas like Les Madrigueres Nature Reserve.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Vendrell?
A: El Vendrell is suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring both coastal and inland trails. Summer can be warm, especially for inland ascents, but coastal walks can be refreshing. Winter provides cooler, often clear days, ideal for longer hikes.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for more experienced hikers, there are challenging routes, with 8 tours rated as difficult. The Serra del Montmell, for example, features sections that can be stony and steep, requiring good fitness. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343093645/pr-c-12-el-vendrell-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR C 12: El Vendrell Trail&lt;/a&gt;, at 8.3 miles (13.3 km), offer a moderate challenge through olive groves and historical sites.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access hiking trailheads?
A: While specific public transport details for every trailhead are not provided, El Vendrell is a municipality with public transport connections. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or train services to nearby towns for access to trail starting points, especially for routes that are not directly within the town center.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical sites or ruins?
A: Yes, several trails in El Vendrell incorporate historical elements. Inland routes often pass through areas with historical sites, such as the ruins of the medieval Castellot and the 11th-century Romanesque hermitage of Sant Miquel in the Serra del Montmell. The 'Old Sanatorium of Sant Joan de Déu' is another historical site accessible via certain paths.
tips: Here you will find a nice boardwalk next to the beach. In summer it can be completely crowded, but on an off-season day, it's really nice to complement a longer walk or just come, sit and look out to sea.
This is a mostly paved road but has little to no traffic. It passes through nice wooded areas and there are several smaller trails that cross it and lead to other interesting areas.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 8.87 kilometers long and can typically be completed in about 2 hours and 21 minutes. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this trail?
faqs.answer: This hike is generally considered moderate. It features easily-accessible paths, including a significant portion along a boardwalk next to the beach. While suitable for most skill levels, good fitness may be beneficial for the full 8.87 km distance.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers pleasant coastal views, especially along the Coma-Ruga Beach Promenade. You'll also pass by the Carrer Lleure and Stone Hut Picnic Area, which could be a nice spot for a break.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially in more populated areas like the promenade, and be mindful of local regulations regarding dogs on beaches or in specific areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: While enjoyable year-round, the off-season is often more pleasant. The boardwalk section along the beach can become quite crowded during the summer months. Visiting outside of peak tourist season will offer a more tranquil experience.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access or hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the Passeig Marítim de Coma-Ruga. You should be able to find parking options along or near the promenade in Coma-Ruga. Availability might vary, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with any other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will encounter parts of the Catalunya E24, Camí dels Masos, Camí dels Cebers, and Carrer dels Trovadors along the way.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Given its starting point along the Passeig Marítim de Coma-Ruga, you will find various cafes and restaurants along the beachfront promenade in Coma-Ruga, perfect for a refreshment before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for families with children?
faqs.answer: With its moderate difficulty, easily-accessible paths, and coastal scenery, this circular route can be suitable for families. The boardwalk section is particularly easy for children. Consider the full 8.87 km distance for younger children and plan for breaks.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the Camí de la Costa Roja section?
faqs.answer: While specific details for the 'Camí de la Costa Roja' section are not extensively described, the name suggests elements of a 'Red Coast,' which typically implies rocky, reddish coastal landscapes. You can expect continued coastal scenery and potentially more rugged natural features compared to the promenade.

waypoints.name: Carrer Lleure and Stone Hut Picnic Area
